paragraphs.en: My first visit to London left me with a deep impression of its orderly yet vibrant streets. The double-decker buses moved with surprising precision, and the polite queues at every bus stop were a testament to British courtesy. It was a world where tradition and modernity coexisted harmoniously.
paragraphs.zh: 我第一次访问伦敦，对其井然有序却又充满活力的街道留下了深刻印象。双层巴士以惊人的精准度行驶，每个公交车站礼貌的排队队伍都是英国式礼貌的明证。这是一个传统与现代和谐共存的世界。

vocabulary.example: The orderly flow of traffic impressed the visitors.
His success is a testament to his hard work.
Different cultures coexist peacefully in this city.

difficult_sentences.en: The double-decker buses moved with surprising precision, and the polite queues at every bus stop were a testament to British courtesy.
difficult_sentences.zh: 双层巴士以惊人的精准度行驶，每个公交车站礼貌的排队队伍都是英国式礼貌的明证。
difficult_sentences.grammar: 这是一个由and连接的并列句。前半句主语为The double-decker buses，谓语moved，介词短语with surprising precision作方式状语；后半句主语为the polite queues，系动词were，表语为a testament to British courtesy，介词短语at every bus stop作定语修饰queues。
difficult_sentences.pattern: N + V + with + adj. + N, and + N + be + a testament to + N.
difficult_sentences.note: a testament to 是固定搭配，表示‘...的证明’，常用于正式或文学语境。

chunks.en: My first visit to London
chunks.zh: 我第一次访问伦敦

chunks.en: left me with a deep impression
chunks.zh: 给我留下了深刻印象

chunks.en: of its orderly yet vibrant streets
chunks.zh: 对其井然有序却又充满活力的街道

chunks.en: The double-decker buses moved with surprising precision
chunks.zh: 双层巴士以惊人的精准度行驶

chunks.en: and the polite queues at every bus stop were a testament to British courtesy
chunks.zh: 每个公交车站礼貌的排队队伍都是英国式礼貌的明证

points.explanation: 由and连接两个独立的句子，表示并列关系。在描述连续的现象时很常用。
points.example_sentence: The double-decker buses moved with surprising precision, and the polite queues at every bus stop were a testament to British courtesy.
points.common_mistake: ❌ 使用逗号连接两个独立句子（逗号拼接）→ ✅ 使用and或分号连接。
points.contrast: and（并列）vs. but（转折）：and表顺承，but表对比。这里两个事实都是正面描述，用and更合适。

paragraphs.en: One afternoon, I was invited to experience a traditional English afternoon tea. The three-tiered stand was laden with delicate sandwiches, scones with clotted cream, and exquisite pastries. As I sipped the steaming Earl Grey tea, I realized that this leisurely ritual was far more than a meal — it was a celebration of social connection.
paragraphs.zh: 一天下午，我受邀体验了传统的英式下午茶。三层架子上摆满了精致的三明治、配凝脂奶油的司康饼和精美的糕点。当我啜饮着热气腾腾的伯爵茶时，我意识到这种悠闲的仪式远不止是一顿饭——它是对社交联结的庆祝。

vocabulary.example: The table was laden with delicious food.
Morning coffee is a daily ritual for many people.
The artist created an exquisite sculpture.

difficult_sentences.en: As I sipped the steaming Earl Grey tea, I realized that this leisurely ritual was far more than a meal — it was a celebration of social connection.
difficult_sentences.zh: 当我啜饮着热气腾腾的伯爵茶时，我意识到这种悠闲的仪式远不止是一顿饭——它是对社交联结的庆祝。
difficult_sentences.grammar: 主句为I realized，后接that引导的宾语从句；从句中主干是this leisurely ritual was far more than a meal，破折号后为补充说明。As引导时间状语从句，表示‘当…时’。
difficult_sentences.pattern: As + 主语 + 动词 + 宾语, 主语 + realized + that + 主语 + was far more than + 名词 — it was + 名词短语.
difficult_sentences.note: far more than 意为‘远不止’，比much more than语气更强。破折号常用来引出解释或强调，书面语中常见。

chunks.en: One afternoon
chunks.zh: 一天下午

chunks.en: I was invited to experience a traditional English afternoon tea
chunks.zh: 我受邀体验了传统的英式下午茶

chunks.en: The three-tiered stand was laden with delicate sandwiches, scones with clotted cream, and exquisite pastries
chunks.zh: 三层架子上摆满了精致的三明治、配凝脂奶油的司康饼和精美的糕点

chunks.en: As I sipped the steaming Earl Grey tea
chunks.zh: 当我啜饮着热气腾腾的伯爵茶时

chunks.en: I realized that this leisurely ritual was far more than a meal — it was a celebration of social connection
chunks.zh: 我意识到这种悠闲的仪式远不止是一顿饭——它是对社交联结的庆祝

points.explanation: 由as引导，表示主句动作发生时伴随的动作。as强调两个动作同时发生，比when更强调伴随。
points.example_sentence: As I sipped the steaming Earl Grey tea, I realized that this leisurely ritual was far more than a meal.
points.common_mistake: ❌ As I was sipping, I realized... 时态无误，但若主从句都用一般过去时也是可以的。常见错误：用when代替as，但when更侧重时间点，as侧重背景动作。
points.contrast: as（强调同时）vs. when（表示时间点）vs. while（强调持续过程，常与进行时连用）。

paragraphs.en: During my stay, I also visited the British Museum. Standing in the Great Court, I was overwhelmed by the sheer scale of human history. The Rosetta Stone and the Parthenon sculptures were not merely artifacts — they bore witness to civilizations long past. To walk among them was to travel through time.
paragraphs.zh: 逗留期间，我还参观了大英博物馆。站在大中庭，我被人类历史的宏大规模所震撼。罗塞塔石碑和帕特农神庙雕塑不仅仅是文物——它们见证了早已逝去的文明。走在它们中间，就如同穿越时空。

vocabulary.meaning: adj. 被压倒的；不知所措的
vocabulary.example: She was overwhelmed by the beauty of the landscape.
The museum displays artifacts from ancient Egypt.
The ruins bear witness to a once-great civilization.

difficult_sentences.en: The Rosetta Stone and the Parthenon sculptures were not merely artifacts — they bore witness to civilizations long past.
difficult_sentences.zh: 罗塞塔石碑和帕特农神庙雕塑不仅仅是文物——它们见证了早已逝去的文明。
difficult_sentences.grammar: 这是一个并列句，由破折号连接。前半句主语为The Rosetta Stone and the Parthenon sculptures，系动词were，表语为not merely artifacts；后半句主语they，谓语bore witness to，宾语civilizations long past（long past为形容词短语后置作定语）。
difficult_sentences.pattern: 主语 + be + not merely + 名词 — 主语 + bear witness to + 名词.
difficult_sentences.note: not merely… — … 相当于 not only…but also… 的强调形式。bear witness to 是正式短语，相当于 testify to。

chunks.en: During my stay
chunks.zh: 逗留期间

chunks.en: I also visited the British Museum
chunks.zh: 我还参观了大英博物馆

chunks.en: Standing in the Great Court
chunks.zh: 站在大中庭
chunks.function: 现在分词短语作时间状语

chunks.en: I was overwhelmed by the sheer scale of human history
chunks.zh: 我被人类历史的宏大规模所震撼

chunks.en: The Rosetta Stone and the Parthenon sculptures were not merely artifacts — they bore witness to civilizations long past
chunks.zh: 罗塞塔石碑和帕特农神庙雕塑不仅仅是文物——它们见证了早已逝去的文明

points.explanation: Standing in the Great Court是现在分词短语，作时间状语，表示主句动作发生时的伴随状态。相当于When I stood in the Great Court。
points.example_sentence: Standing in the Great Court, I was overwhelmed by the sheer scale of human history.
points.common_mistake: ❌ 使用过去分词Standed → ✅ 现在分词Standing。注意分词逻辑主语要与主句主语一致（I）。
points.contrast: 现在分词（主动）vs. 过去分词（被动）：如Standing（我站）vs. Surrounded（我被包围）。

paragraphs.en: Reflecting on my trip, I concluded that travel is not merely about seeing new places — it is a profound encounter with different ways of life. British reserve surprised me at first, but soon I came to appreciate its underlying warmth. Perhaps the greatest lesson I learned was to embrace unfamiliar customs with an open mind.
paragraphs.zh: 回顾我的旅行，我得出结论：旅行不仅仅是看新地方——它是对不同生活方式的深刻邂逅。起初英国人的内敛让我惊讶，但很快我开始欣赏它暗藏的温暖。或许我学到的最大的教训是以开放的心态接纳不熟悉的习俗。

vocabulary.meaning: adj. 深刻的；意义深远的
vocabulary.example: The book had a profound impact on me.
The British are known for their reserve.
vocabulary.derivatives: reserve (v./n.) → reserved (adj.) → unreserved (adj.) 无保留的

difficult_sentences.en: Reflecting on my trip, I concluded that travel is not merely about seeing new places — it is a profound encounter with different ways of life.
difficult_sentences.zh: 回顾我的旅行，我得出结论：旅行不仅仅是看新地方——它是对不同生活方式的深刻邂逅。
difficult_sentences.grammar: Reflecting on my trip为现在分词短语作时间状语。主句为I concluded，后接that引导的宾语从句。从句中主干是travel is not merely about seeing new places，破折号后补充说明，it指代travel。
difficult_sentences.pattern: Reflecting on + 名词, 主语 + concluded that + 主语 + be not merely about + 名词 — it be + 名词 + with + 名词.
difficult_sentences.note: reflect on 意为‘反思’；encounter with 是固定搭配，意为‘与…的相遇’；be not merely about 比 be not only about 更正式。

chunks.en: Reflecting on my trip
chunks.zh: 回顾我的旅行
chunks.function: 时间状语（现在分词短语）

chunks.en: I concluded that travel is not merely about seeing new places
chunks.zh: 我得出结论：旅行不仅仅是看新地方

chunks.en: — it is a profound encounter with different ways of life
chunks.zh: ——它是对不同生活方式的深刻邂逅

chunks.en: British reserve surprised me at first
chunks.zh: 起初英国人的内敛让我惊讶

chunks.en: but soon I came to appreciate its underlying warmth
chunks.zh: 但很快我开始欣赏它暗藏的温暖

chunks.en: Perhaps the greatest lesson I learned was to embrace unfamiliar customs with an open mind
chunks.zh: 或许我学到的最大的教训是以开放的心态接纳不熟悉的习俗
chunks.function: 主句（表语为不定式短语）

points.explanation: 在the greatest lesson I learned was to embrace...中，不定式短语to embrace...作表语，说明lesson的内容。这种结构用于定义或解释主语。
points.example_sentence: Perhaps the greatest lesson I learned was to embrace unfamiliar customs with an open mind.
points.common_mistake: ❌ 用动名词：was embracing → ✅ 不定式：was to embrace。不定式更强调目的或未来。
points.contrast: 不定式作表语（to do）vs. 动名词作表语（doing）：前者侧重具体动作或尚未发生，后者侧重习惯或一般事实。例如：My job is to teach.（具体任务）vs. My job is teaching.（职业性质）。
